title: machilus curranii
intro: Machilus curranii, also known as the Philippine Avocado or Mountain Avocado, is a species of plant in the laurel family native to the Philippines. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The plant is valued for its health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. The herbal preparations of Machilus curranii include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, and topical creams for various health purposes.
